sentences.text: Hey, good to, how are you?
Looking forward to a bit of time off coming up.
You, you can see I'm in a turtleneck, so I'm up here in uh Chicago.
Well, I, I know we have a few components to discuss today.
Where, where do you want to start?

I know you attended our webinar last week and there's some interest in the research component and then there's a uh business intelligence side of this as well, just kind of liberating the data out of IQ.

yeah, so um re I mean, e either one obviously with pulling the data out, um It would be nice maybe to be able to extract that, you know, bump it into Power Bi I um And then the powers that be could use Power Bi I to look at the data as well.
And then obviously we can have some historical data um sort of thing.
So just curious to see how that works, how that looks.

Um And then the research component, um just thought that was kind of interesting and just wanted to get a little bit more detail on that to see if it's something that, you know, maybe we could use uh going forward as well for, you know, rent comps for, you know, if we're looking in certain areas um um you know, that that would basically go be what we want to use it for.
Let let's start with the data side of things.
So um intention, it sounds like it's pushed to be uh you had mentioned a uh API would you be open to a uh end link instead?
Um No, I'm not sure what that is.
So it's a much shorter uh integration.
You're gonna essentially get the, the same uh data with it.
It's gonna be updated uh daily for you.
So, um when you look at IQ,
so it's a simple, essentially, it's like a 7 to 10 step integration with B I, we've got about 30% of our customer base on it.

Uh We just provide a URL that's specific to Franklin Street and then it's gonna update the data uh for you every single day and that's gonna happen at a uh a property level, a floor plan level and an individual unit level.
And so I can send you the data glossary following this of just what data points would be in there.

But essentially when you think about it, um if you've seen this Excel report where it's got property performance and then floor plan level data and then individual units, uh that's all the data that's gonna be pushed to you on a daily basis.
So a little bit easier or a little bit less cost prohibitive to set it up that way.
Uh I can send you an integration guide on that just to make sure that that uh checks out for you.
Yeah, yeah, that would be great.
And then uh research side of things, it, it sounds like uh that was demoed live on the webinar.
So you want me to walk through anything in, in particular there?
Um No, I mean, I think I saw a good bit of it.
I, I guess um just trying to think anything.
So it would be we could basically pick any market we wanted to like, I guess is there any restrictions or how, how does it work?

You know, we think like, hey, we wanna look at the Charlotte market or we wanna look at Chicago or we wanna look at the Midwest, you know, somewhere else in the Midwest or out west or like, how, how does it, are we paying per market or are we just paying and then we get to see whatever we want.
So it's access to all 3 million properties.
Um The only restriction on that is by uh users.
So the first kind of cost bracket that we have with research is uh, up to five users.
And then the second bracket is just, uh, uh, up to 10 users, $25,000 annually and that can be paid out on a monthly basis.
Um but it's just restricted by number of seats that we permission for it.
And then what's interesting in your case is, so data is an incremental 20 cents per unit.
So to set up that feed,
but we don't do it unless there's a uh a minimum uh spend of $1000 monthly.
So in your scenario, it would be um if we're bringing research on that's gonna put us over that threshold.
So I think we're around like $500 monthly or something of that effect right now.
And then enabling data would cost like an extra couple of $100.
Um So it's just a decision on, you know, if you want to bring that research component on,
otherwise we can try and flat rate it for you at 1000 monthly.
But um that's the only restriction on that, that data component of things
So I can outline all that for you just like in an email.
I just wanted to make sure uh we didn't need to go over anything technically here, I'll send you the data glossary just so you know what data points you'll be getting.
Um but just know like it's gonna be all the data you already have access to in here just just pulled out I think it's about 45 or so data points that refresh every single day for you in your B I.
Perfect that's all I need um

I'll get your inbox let me know if you have any any questions or if you wanna move forward and then if you do wanna move forward I think what would what would be best to just do is put it uh all on one contract starting 11 2025 and we'll get in each one of those, each one of those components.
